meaning: n.卫戍部队,驻军,卫戍地,要塞v.守卫,驻防
examples: According to Webb, the purpose of garrison government was to provide military support for a royal policy designed to limit the power of the upper classes in the American colonies.
Two regiments were sent to garrison the town.
The approaches to the garrison have been heavily mined.
Fort Bliss Public Affairs and Garrison Command approved the shoot, an Army spokesman said.
Early Australia (ie, Sydney) was exclusively convict, under the control of a military garrison.
